Tests the conversion of an empty object to a Boolean value in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the truthiness of an empty object in JavaScript. It uses the `assert` function to verify that the expression `true === Boolean({})` evaluates to `true`. In JavaScript, an empty object is considered a truthy value, and the `Boolean` function converts it to `true`. This script serves as a basic test to confirm the expected behavior of type conversion in JavaScript.
